paper_title: Sclerobiont Colonization Patterns on Mollusk Assemblages

methods.description: This method investigates the patterns of sclerobiont colonization on mollusk assemblages, considering factors such as shell color, size, and taphonomic alterations. The study aims to understand how these factors influence the preservation and colonization of sclerobionts on mollusk shells.

steps.description: Analyze the effect of shell color on sclerobiont colonization, comparing oxidized and non-oxidized shells.
steps.searchable_summary: compare sclerobiont colonization on oxidized vs. non-oxidized shells
outputs: correlation between shell color and colonization

steps.description: Examine the relationship between shell size and sclerobiont colonization, considering both encrustation and bioerosion.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze correlation between shell size and sclerobiont colonization
outputs: correlation between shell size and colonization

steps.description: Investigate the impact of taphonomic alterations on the preservation of sclerobiont traces on shells.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze impact of taphonomic alterations on sclerobiont preservation
outputs: impact of taphonomic alterations on preservation

steps.description: Assess the influence of external ornamentation on sclerobiont colonization and its correlation with taphonomic damage.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze correlation between external ornamentation and sclerobiont colonization

steps.description: Evaluate the role of shell mineralogy and life mode in sclerobiont colonization patterns.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate impact of shell mineralogy and life mode on colonization
outputs: impact of mineralogy and life mode on colonization
